title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

title: LEX Baza Dokumentów is vulnerable to DOM-based XSS in "em" cookie parameter. The application...
description:
LEX Baza Dokumentów is vulnerable to DOM-based XSS in "em" cookie parameter. The application unsafely
processes the parameter on the client side, allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary
JavaScript in the context of the victim's browser.
An attacker with ability to set a cookie can perform a more severe attack, so we evaluate the impact and risk of exploitation as minimal. However, the vendor considered this a vulnerability and released a security patch.

This issue was fixed in version 1.3.4.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the victim's browser, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions within the application. However, exploitation requires the ability to set a cookie, reducing the likelihood of widespread impact.
recommended_actions: Upgrade LEX Baza Dokumentów to version 1.3.4 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to reduce the risk of malicious script execution.
Educate users on the importance of applying security patches promptly.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

cifs: some missing...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

cifs: some missing initializations on replay

In several places in the code, we have a label to signify
the start of the code where a request can be replayed if
necessary. However, some of these places were missing the
necessary reinitializations of certain local variables
before replay.

This change makes sure that these variables get initialized
after the label.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's CIFS implementation could allow attackers to exploit improperly initialized variables during request replays, potentially leading to data corruption, unauthorized access, or denial of service in systems relying on CIFS for file sharing.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest Linux kernel patches that address CVE-2026-31693 to ensure the vulnerability is resolved.
Audit systems using CIFS for file sharing to identify potential exposure and mitigate risks.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity related to CIFS operations and implement additional security measures such as network segmentation.

title:
Dancer::Session::Abstract versions through 1.3522 for Perl generates session ids insecurely.

The...
description:
Dancer::Session::Abstract versions through 1.3522 for Perl generates session ids insecurely.

The session id is generated from summing the character codepoints of the absolute pathname with the process id, the epoch time and calls to the built-in rand() function to return a number between 0 and 999-billion, and concatenating that result three times.

The path name might be known or guessed by an attacker, especially for applications known to be written using Dancer with standard installation loc

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5080.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

rtnetlink: add missing...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

rtnetlink: add missing netlink_ns_capable() check for peer netns

rtnl_newlink() lacks a CAP_NET_ADMIN capability check on the peer
network namespace when creating paired devices (veth, vxcan,
netkit). This allows an unprivileged user with a user namespace
to create interfaces in arbitrary network namespaces, including
init_net.

Add a netlink_ns_capable() check for CAP_NET_ADMIN in the peer
namespace before allowing device cre

description: The Five Star Restaurant Reservations plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to a payment bypass via PHP type juggling in versions up to, and including, 2.7.16 This is due to the valid_payment() function using a PHP loose comparison (==) between the attacker-controlled payment_id POST parameter and the booking's stripe_payment_intent_id property. When an unauthenticated attacker submits a request to the nopriv AJAX handler rtb_stripe_pmt_succeed before the Stripe payment intent has been created for

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6498.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

xen/privcmd: fix double free...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

xen/privcmd: fix double free via VMA splitting

privcmd_vm_ops defines .close (privcmd_close), but neither .may_split
nor .open. When userspace does a partial munmap() on a privcmd mapping,
the kernel splits the VMA via __split_vma(). Since may_split is NULL,
the split is allowed. vm_area_dup() copies vm_private_data (a pages
array allocated in alloc_empty_pages()) into the new VMA without any
fixup, because there is no .open c

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability involves a double free issue in the Linux kernel's xen/privcmd module, which could lead to memory corruption, potential privilege escalation, or denial of service attacks. Exploitation could allow attackers to destabilize systems or execute arbitrary code.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest Linux kernel version where the vulnerability has been patched (check for CVE-2026-31787 fixes).
Audit systems running Xen hypervisors to ensure they are not exposed to untrusted users or workloads.
Implement additional monitoring to detect unusual activity related to memory management or VMA operations.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

Buffer overflow in drivers...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

Buffer overflow in drivers/xen/sys-hypervisor.c

The build id returned by HYPERVISOR_xen_version(XENVER_build_id) is
neither NUL terminated nor a string.

The first causes a buffer overflow as sprintf in buildid_show will
read and copy till it finds a NUL.

00000000  f4 91 51 f4 dd 38 9e 9d  65 47 52 eb 10 71 db 50  |..Q..8..eGR..q.P|
00000010  b9 a8 01 42 6f 2e 32                              |...Bo.2|
00000017

So use a memcp

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The buffer overflow vulnerability in the Linux kernel's Xen hypervisor driver could allow attackers to exploit memory corruption, potentially leading to privilege escalation, denial of service, or arbitrary code execution.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-31786.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using Xen hypervisor and apply mitigations, such as disabling unnecessary hypervisor features.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

2025.1.7.1,

2025.3.4.1, 
2026.1.1 reading arbitrary local files was possible via built-in web server

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthorized access to arbitrary local files via the built-in web server in JetBrains IntelliJ IDEA, potentially exposing sensitive information such as credentials, configuration files, or intellectual property.
recommended_actions: Immediately update JetBrains IntelliJ IDEA to the latest patched version (2024.3.7.1, 2025.1.7.1, 2025.2.6.2, 2025.3.4.1, or 2026.1.1) to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a review of access logs to identify any unauthorized access attempts or suspicious activity that may have exploited this vulnerability.
Implement network segmentation and restrict access to the built-in web server to trusted IP ranges to minimize exposure.

title: Apache Airflow's SMTP provider `SmtpHook` called Python's `smtplib.SMTP.starttls()` without an...
description: Apache Airflow's SMTP provider `SmtpHook` called Python's `smtplib.SMTP.starttls()` without an SSL context, so no certificate validation was performed on the TLS upgrade. A man-in-the-middle between the Airflow worker and the SMTP server could present a self-signed certificate, complete the STARTTLS upgrade, and capture the SMTP credentials sent during the subsequent `login()` call. Users are advised to upgrade to the `apache-airflow-providers-smtp` version that contains the fix.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41016.

title: Incorrect packet validation allowed unbounded recursion parsing SCTP chunk parameters.  This can...
description:
Incorrect packet validation allowed unbounded recursion parsing SCTP chunk parameters.  This can eventually result in a stack overflow and panic.

Remote attackers can craft packets which cause affected systems to panic.  This affects any system where pf is configured to process traffic, independent of the configured ruleset.

title:
Out-of-bounds read vulnerability in ASR Kestrel (nr_fw modules) allows Overflow Buffers.

 This...
description:
Out-of-bounds read vulnerability in ASR Kestrel (nr_fw modules) allows Overflow Buffers.

 This vulnerability is associated with program files Code/Nr/nr_fw/RA/src/NrPwrCtrl.C.



This issue affects Kestrel: before 2026/02/10.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The out-of-bounds read vulnerability in ASR Kestrel's nr_fw modules could allow attackers to exploit overflow buffers, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or system crashes. This could compromise the integrity and availability of affected systems.
recommended_actions: Immediately update ASR Kestrel firmware to the latest version released after 2026/02/10 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough vulnerability assessment of systems using ASR Kestrel to identify and address potential exploitation risks.
Implement monitoring and intrusion detection systems to detect any suspicious activity targeting the nr_fw modules.

title: When processing the header of an incoming message, libnv failed to properly validate the message...
description:
When processing the header of an incoming message, libnv failed to properly validate the message size.

The lack of validation allows a malicious program to write outside the bounds of a heap allocation.  This can trigger a crash or system panic, and it may be possible for an unprivileged user to exploit the bug to elevate their privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-35547.

title: When exchanging data over a socket, libnv uses select(2) to wait for data to arrive.  However, it...
description:
When exchanging data over a socket, libnv uses select(2) to wait for data to arrive.  However, it does not verify whether the provided socket descriptor fits in select(2)'s file descriptor set size limit of FD_SETSIZE (1024).

An attacker who is able to force a libnv application to allocate large file descriptors, e.g., by opening many descriptors and executing a program which is not careful to close them upon startup, can trigger stack corruption.  If the target application is setuid-root, then

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-39457.

title: An operator precedence bug in the kernel results in a scenario where a buffer overflow causes...
description:
An operator precedence bug in the kernel results in a scenario where a buffer overflow causes attacker-controlled data to overwrite adjacent execve(2) argument buffers.

The bug may be exploitable by an unprivileged user to obtain superuser privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7270.

title: As dhclient is building an environment to pass to dhclient-script, it may need to resize the...
description:
As dhclient is building an environment to pass to dhclient-script, it may need to resize the array of string pointers.  The code which expands the array incorrectly calculates its new size when requesting memory, resulting in a heap buffer overrun.

A specially crafted packet can cause dhclient to overrun its buffer of environment entries.  This can result in a crash, but it may be possible to leverage this bug to achieve remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42512.

Restrict access to Wireshark systems and ensure only trusted users can interact with network data.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity that may indicate exploitation attempts.

description: IBM Verify Identity Access Container 11.0 through 11.0.2 and IBM Security Verify Access Container 10.0 through 10.0.9.1 and IBM Verify Identity Access 11.0 through 11.0.2 and IBM Security Verify Access 10.0 through 10.0.9.1 uses weaker than expected cryptographic algorithms that could allow an attacker to decrypt highly sensitive information.

This vulnerability allows an administrative user to embed arbitrary JavaScript code in the Web UI thus altering the intended functionality potentially leading to credentials disclosure within a trusted session.

title: CVE-2026-41179 - Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage p
description: Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage providers. Starting in version 1.48.0 and prior to version 1.73.5, the RC endpoint `operations/fsinfo` is exposed without `AuthRequired: true` and accepts attacker-controlled `fs` input. Because `rc.GetFs(...)` supports inline backend definitions, an unauthenticated attacker can instantiate an attacker-controlled backend on demand. For the WebDAV backend, `bearer_token_command` is executed during backend initialization, making single-request unauthenticated local command execution possible on reachable RC deployments without global HTTP authentication. Version 1.73.5 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41179.

title: CVE-2026-41176 - Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage p
description: Rclone is a command-line program to sync files and directories to and from different cloud storage providers. The RC endpoint `options/set` is exposed without `AuthRequired: true`, but it can mutate global runtime configuration, including the RC option block itself. Starting in version 1.45.0 and prior to version 1.73.5, an unauthenticated attacker can set `rc.NoAuth=true`, which disables the authorization gate for many RC methods registered with `AuthRequired: true` on reachable RC servers that are started without global HTTP authentication. This can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive administrative functionality, including configuration and operational RC methods. Version 1.73.5 patches the issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-41176.

title: CVE-2026-32679 - The installers of LiveOn Meet Client for Windows (Downloader5Installer.exe and Downloader5InstallerF
description: The installers of LiveOn Meet Client for Windows (Downloader5Installer.exe and Downloader5InstallerForAdmin.exe) and the installers of Canon Network Camera Plugin (CanonNWCamPlugin.exe and CanonNWCamPluginForAdmin.exe) insecurely load Dynamic Link Libraries (DLLs).
